api: Braintree Gateway server-side and XML API
scope: Pass B expansion against Braintree's official server-side request index plus the documented XML API examples. Covered streams are the gateway resources that can be represented as ordinary JSON list reads on the legacy /merchants/{merchant_id}/... HTTP shape: transactions, customers, subscriptions, add-ons, discounts, plans, merchant accounts, payment methods, disputes, and Apple Pay registered domains. Braintree's mutating server-side/XML calls are excluded because their documented wire shape is XML or SDK-managed payment/PCI/admin workflows rather than this dialect's JSON/form single-record write body. Detail lookups for resources whose list/search stream returns the same resource family are marked duplicate_of the corresponding stream.

excluded.reason: single transaction lookup by id; the transactions stream covers the same transaction resource family for sync
SDK search method for transactions; represented by the transactions list stream on the legacy gateway HTTP shape
single customer lookup by id; covered by the customers stream's resource shape
SDK customer search method; represented by the customers list stream on the legacy gateway HTTP shape
single subscription lookup by id; covered by the subscriptions stream's resource shape
SDK subscription search method; represented by the subscriptions stream
single plan lookup by id; covered by the plans stream
single merchant-account lookup by id; covered by the merchant_accounts stream
single vaulted payment-method lookup by token; covered by the payment_methods stream
single dispute lookup by id; covered by the disputes stream
date-window lookup for expiring cards; requires caller-supplied start/end dates and does not define a stable list-all sync stream
ad hoc verification search keyed by caller criteria; not a fixed list/detail resource and tied to payment-method verification workflow data
line items are enumerable only under an already-known transaction id; adding a transaction fan_out was deferred for this gateway connector
fee report requires caller-supplied report criteria and returns computed reporting data rather than a stable resource collection
settlement batch summary is a parameterized generated report, not a list/detail collection with a stable record id
